title: Shout Decorator
description:
Define a decorator function called shout that wraps a function. The wrapper should print 'BEFORE' before calling the original function, and 'AFTER' after calling it. Then apply @shout to a function called greet that prints 'Hello!'. Finally, call greet(). Make sure to use the @shout syntax above the greet definition.

Expected output:
BEFORE
Hello!
AFTER
starter_code:
# Define your decorator here

# Apply it to a function and call it

code_requirements.description: Uses the @decorator syntax to apply the decorator
code_requirements.fail_message: Use the @shout syntax above the greet() function definition to apply the decorator.

code_requirements.description: Defines a wrapper function inside the decorator
code_requirements.fail_message: Define a wrapper function inside your decorator that wraps the original function.
Define a function called 'shout' that takes a function as its parameter.

hints: Define shout(func): with an inner def wrapper(): that prints 'BEFORE', calls func(), then prints 'AFTER'
Return the wrapper function from shout without calling it — just return wrapper
Apply it with @shout right above def greet():, which should print 'Hello!'
